NKJV.1: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
NKJV.2: Now early in the morning He came again into the temple, and all the people came to Him; and He sat down and taught them.
NKJV.3: Then the scribes and Pharisees brought to Him a woman caught in adultery. And when they had set her in the midst,
NKJV.4: they said to Him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in adultery, in the very act.
NKJV.5: Now Moses, in the law, commanded us that such should be stoned. But what do You say?”
NKJV.6: This they said, testing Him, that they might have something of which to accuse Him. But Jesus stooped down and wrote on the ground with His finger, as though He did not hear.
NKJV.7: So when they continued asking Him, He raised Himself up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw a stone at her first.”
NKJV.8: And again He stooped down and wrote on the ground.
NKJV.9: Then those who heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning with the oldest even to the last. And Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
NKJV.10: When Jesus had raised Himself up and saw no one but the woman, He said to her, “Woman, where are those accusers of yours? Has no one condemned you?”
NKJV.11: She said, “No one, Lord.” And Jesus said to her, “Neither do I condemn you; go and sin no more.”
NKJV.12: Then Jesus spoke to them again, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who  follows Me shall not walk in darkness, but have the light of life.”
NKJV.13: The Pharisees therefore said to Him, “You bear witness of Yourself; Your witness is not true.”
NKJV.14: Jesus answered and said to them, “Even if I bear witness of Myself, My witness is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going; but  you do not know where I come from and where I am going.
NKJV.15: You judge according to the flesh;  I judge no one.
NKJV.16: And yet if I do judge, My judgment is true; for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent Me.
NKJV.17: It is also written in your law that the testimony of two men is true.
NKJV.18: I am One who bears witness of Myself, and  the Father who sent Me bears witness of Me.”
NKJV.19: Then they said to Him, “Where is Your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither Me nor My Father.  If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also.”
NKJV.20: These words Jesus spoke in the treasury, as He taught in the temple; and no one laid hands on Him, for His hour had not yet come.
NKJV.21: Then Jesus said to them again, “I am going away, and  you will seek Me, and  will die in your sin. Where I go you cannot come.”
NKJV.22: So the Jews said, “Will He kill Himself, because He says, ‘Where I go you cannot come’?”
NKJV.23: And He said to them, “You are from beneath; I am from above.  You are of this world; I am not of this world.
NKJV.24: Therefore I said to you that you will die in your sins; for if you do not believe that I am He, you will die in your sins.”
NKJV.25: Then they said to Him, “Who are You?” And Jesus said to them, “Just what I  have been saying to you from the beginning.
NKJV.26: I have many things to say and to judge concerning you, but  He who sent Me is true; and  I speak to the world those things which I heard from Him.”
NKJV.27: They did not understand that He spoke to them of the Father.
NKJV.28: Then Jesus said to them, “When you lift up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am He, and that I do nothing of Myself; but as My Father taught Me, I speak these things.
NKJV.29: And  He who sent Me is with Me.  The Father has not left Me alone,  for I always do those things that please Him.”
NKJV.30: As He spoke these words, many believed in Him.
NKJV.31: Then Jesus said to those Jews who believed Him, “If you  abide in My word, you are My disciples indeed.
NKJV.32: And you shall know the  truth, and  the truth shall make you free.”
NKJV.33: They answered Him, “We are Abraham’s descendants, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How can You say, ‘You will be made free’?”
NKJV.34: Jesus answered them, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  whoever commits sin is a slave of sin.
NKJV.35: And a slave does not abide in the house forever, but a son abides forever.
NKJV.36: Therefore if the Son makes you free, you shall be free indeed.
NKJV.37: “I know that you are Abraham’s descendants, but  you seek to kill Me, because My word has no place in you.
NKJV.38: I speak what I have seen with My Father, and you do what you have seen with your father.”
NKJV.39: They answered and said to Him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.
NKJV.40: But now you seek to kill Me, a Man who has told you the truth  which I heard from God. Abraham did not do this.
NKJV.41: You do the deeds of your father.” Then they said to Him, “We were not born of fornication; we have one Father—God.”
NKJV.42: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love Me, for  I proceeded forth and came from God;  nor have I come of Myself, but He sent Me.
NKJV.43: Why do you not understand My speech? Because you are not able to listen to My word.
NKJV.44: You are of your father the devil, and the desires of your father you want to do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks from his own resources, for he is a liar and the father of it.
NKJV.45: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe Me.
NKJV.46: Which of you convicts Me of sin? And if I tell the truth, why do you not believe Me?
NKJV.47: He who is of God hears God’s words; therefore you do not hear, because you are not of God.”
NKJV.48: Then the Jews answered and said to Him, “Do we not say rightly that You are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
NKJV.49: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor My Father, and  you dishonor Me.
NKJV.50: And I do not seek My own glory; there is One who seeks and judges.
NKJV.51: Most assuredly, I say to you,  if anyone keeps My word he shall never see death.”
NKJV.52: Then the Jews said to Him, “Now we know that You have a demon! Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and You say, ‘If anyone keeps My word he shall never taste death.’
NKJV.53: Are You greater than our father Abraham, who is dead? And the prophets are dead. Who do You make Yourself out to be?”
NKJV.54: Jesus answered, “If I honor Myself, My honor is nothing.  It is My Father who honors Me, of whom you say that He is your God.
NKJV.55: Yet  you have not known Him, but I know Him. And if I say, ‘I do not know Him,’ I shall be a liar like you; but I do know Him and  keep His word.
NKJV.56: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day, and he saw it and was glad.”
NKJV.57: Then the Jews said to Him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have You seen Abraham?”
NKJV.58: Jesus said to them, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  before Abraham was,  I AM.”
NKJV.59: Then they took up stones to throw at Him; but Jesus hid Himself and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so passed by.

ESV.1: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
ESV.2: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him, and he sat down and taught them.
ESV.3: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery, and placing her in the midst
ESV.4: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman has been caught in the act of adultery.
ESV.5: Now in the Law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. So what do you say?”
ESV.6: This they said to test him, that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
ESV.7: And as they continued to ask him, he stood up and said to them,  “Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone at her.”
ESV.8: And once more he bent down and wrote on the ground.
ESV.9: But when they heard it, they went away one by one, beginning with the older ones, and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
ESV.10: Jesus stood up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
ESV.11: She said, “No one, Lord.” And Jesus said,  “Neither do I condemn you; go, and from now on sin no more.”]]
ESV.12: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying,  “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.”
ESV.13: So the Pharisees said to him, “You are bearing witness about yourself; your testimony is not true.”
ESV.14: Jesus answered, “Even if I do bear witness about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
ESV.15: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
ESV.16: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father  who sent me.
ESV.17: In your Law it is written that the testimony of two people is true.
ESV.18: I am the one who bears witness about myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness about me.”
ESV.19: They said to him therefore, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered,  “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
ESV.20: These words he spoke in the treasury, as he taught in the temple; but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
ESV.21: So he said to them again,  “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
ESV.22: So the Jews said, “Will he kill himself, since he says, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
ESV.23: He said to them,  “You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
ESV.24: I told you that you would die in your sins, for unless you believe that I am he you will die in your sins.”
ESV.25: So they said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been telling you from the beginning.
ESV.26: I have much to say about you and much to judge, but he who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him.”
ESV.27: They did not understand that he had been speaking to them about the Father.
ESV.28: So Jesus said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just as the Father taught me.
ESV.29: And he who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”
ESV.30: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
ESV.31: So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed him,  “If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples,
ESV.32: and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
ESV.33: They answered him, “We are offspring of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone. How is it that you say, ‘You will become free’?”
ESV.34: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave  to sin.
ESV.35: The slave does not remain in the house forever; the son remains forever.
ESV.36: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
ESV.37: I know that you are offspring of Abraham; yet you seek to kill me because my word finds no place in you.
ESV.38: I speak of what I have seen with my Father, and you do what you have heard from your father.”
ESV.39: They answered him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them,  “If you were Abraham's children, you would be doing the works Abraham did,
ESV.40: but now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.
ESV.41: You are doing the works your father did.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father — even God.”
ESV.42: Jesus said to them,  “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and I am here. I came not of my own accord, but he sent me.
ESV.43: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot bear to hear my word.
ESV.44: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
ESV.45: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
ESV.46: Which one of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
ESV.47: Whoever is of God hears the words of God. The reason why you do not hear them is that you are not of God.”
ESV.48: The Jews answered him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
ESV.49: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
ESV.50: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is One who seeks it, and he is the judge.
ESV.51: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.”
ESV.52: The Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon! Abraham died, as did the prophets, yet you say, ‘If anyone keeps my word, he will never taste death.’
ESV.53: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? And the prophets died! Who do you make yourself out to be?”
ESV.54: Jesus answered,  “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, ‘He is our God.’
ESV.55: But you have not known him. I know him. If I were to say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you, but I do know him and I keep his word.
ESV.56: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad.”
ESV.57: So the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
ESV.58: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.”
ESV.59: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.

NLT.1: Jesus returned to the Mount of Olives,
NLT.2: but early the next morning he was back again at the Temple. A crowd soon gathered, and he sat down and taught them.
NLT.3: As he was speaking, the teachers of religious law and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in the act of adultery. They put her in front of the crowd.
NLT.4: “Teacher,” they said to Jesus, “this woman was caught in the act of adultery.
NLT.5: The law of Moses says to stone her. What do you say?”
NLT.6: They were trying to trap him into saying something they could use against him, but Jesus stooped down and wrote in the dust with his finger.
NLT.7: They kept demanding an answer, so he stood up again and said, “All right, but let the one who has never sinned throw the first stone!”
NLT.8: Then he stooped down again and wrote in the dust.
NLT.9: When the accusers heard this, they slipped away one by one, beginning with the oldest, until only Jesus was left in the middle of the crowd with the woman.
NLT.10: Then Jesus stood up again and said to the woman, “Where are your accusers? Didn’t even one of them condemn you?”
NLT.11: “No, Lord,” she said. And Jesus said, “Neither do I. Go and sin no more.”
NLT.12: Jesus spoke to the people once more and said, “I am the light of the world. If you follow me, you won’t have to walk in darkness, because you will have the light that leads to life.”
NLT.13: The Pharisees replied, “You are making those claims about yourself! Such testimony is not valid.”
NLT.14: Jesus told them, “These claims are valid even though I make them about myself. For I know where I came from and where I am going, but you don’t know this about me.
NLT.15: You judge me by human standards, but I do not judge anyone.
NLT.16: And if I did, my judgment would be correct in every respect because I am not alone. The Father who sent me is with me.
NLT.17: Your own law says that if two people agree about something, their witness is accepted as fact.
NLT.18: I am one witness, and my Father who sent me is the other.”
NLT.19: “Where is your father?” they asked. Jesus answered, “Since you don’t know who I am, you don’t know who my Father is. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.”
NLT.20: Jesus made these statements while he was teaching in the section of the Temple known as the Treasury. But he was not arrested, because his time had not yet come.
NLT.21: Later Jesus said to them again, “I am going away. You will search for me but will die in your sin. You cannot come where I am going.”
NLT.22: The people asked, “Is he planning to commit suicide? What does he mean, ‘You cannot come where I am going’?”
NLT.23: Jesus continued, “You are from below; I am from above. You belong to this world; I do not.
NLT.24: That is why I said that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I AM who I claim to be, you will die in your sins.”
NLT.25: “Who are you?” they demanded. Jesus replied, “The one I have always claimed to be.
NLT.26: I have much to say about you and much to condemn, but I won’t. For I say only what I have heard from the one who sent me, and he is completely truthful.”
NLT.27: But they still didn’t understand that he was talking about his Father.
NLT.28: So Jesus said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man on the cross, then you will understand that I AM he. I do nothing on my own but say only what the Father taught me.
NLT.29: And the one who sent me is with me — he has not deserted me. For I always do what pleases him.”
NLT.30: Then many who heard him say these things believed in him.
NLT.31: Jesus said to the people who believed in him, “You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teachings.
NLT.32: And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
NLT.33: “But we are descendants of Abraham,” they said. “We have never been slaves to anyone. What do you mean, ‘You will be set free’?”
NLT.34: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, everyone who sins is a slave of sin.
NLT.35: A slave is not a permanent member of the family, but a son is part of the family forever.
NLT.36: So if the Son sets you free, you are truly free.
NLT.37: Yes, I realize that you are descendants of Abraham. And yet some of you are trying to kill me because there’s no room in your hearts for my message.
NLT.38: I am telling you what I saw when I was with my Father. But you are following the advice of your father.”
NLT.39: “Our father is Abraham!” they declared. “No,” Jesus replied, “for if you were really the children of Abraham, you would follow his example.
NLT.40: Instead, you are trying to kill me because I told you the truth, which I heard from God. Abraham never did such a thing.
NLT.41: No, you are imitating your real father.” They replied, “We aren’t illegitimate children! God himself is our true Father.”
NLT.42: Jesus told them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, because I have come to you from God. I am not here on my own, but he sent me.
NLT.43: Why can’t you understand what I am saying? It’s because you can’t even hear me!
NLT.44: For you are the children of your father the devil, and you love to do the evil things he does. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has always hated the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, it is consistent with his character; for he is a liar and the father of lies.
NLT.45: So when I tell the truth, you just naturally don’t believe me!
NLT.46: Which of you can truthfully accuse me of sin? And since I am telling you the truth, why don’t you believe me?
NLT.47: Anyone who belongs to God listens gladly to the words of God. But you don’t listen because you don’t belong to God.”
NLT.48: The people retorted, “You Samaritan devil! Didn’t we say all along that you were possessed by a demon?”
NLT.49: “No,” Jesus said, “I have no demon in me. For I honor my Father — and you dishonor me.
NLT.50: And though I have no wish to glorify myself, God is going to glorify me. He is the true judge.
NLT.51: I tell you the truth, anyone who obeys my teaching will never die!”
NLT.52: The people said, “Now we know you are possessed by a demon. Even Abraham and the prophets died, but you say, ‘Anyone who obeys my teaching will never die!’
NLT.53: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
NLT.54: Jesus answered, “If I want glory for myself, it doesn’t count. But it is my Father who will glorify me. You say, ‘He is our God,’
NLT.55: but you don’t even know him. I know him. If I said otherwise, I would be as great a liar as you! But I do know him and obey him.
NLT.56: Your father Abraham rejoiced as he looked forward to my coming. He saw it and was glad.”
NLT.57: The people said, “You aren’t even fifty years old. How can you say you have seen Abraham? ”
NLT.58: Jesus answered, “I tell you the truth, before Abraham was even born, I AM! ”
NLT.59: At that point they picked up stones to throw at him. But Jesus was hidden from them and left the Temple.

WEB.1: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
WEB.2: Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down, and taught them.
WEB.3: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the middle,
WEB.4: they told him, “Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.
WEB.5: Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such women.  What then do you say about her?”
WEB.6: They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of. But Jesus stooped down, and wrote on the ground with his finger.
WEB.7: But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her.”
WEB.8: Again he stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground.
WEB.9: They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.
WEB.10: Jesus, standing up, saw her and said, “Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?”
WEB.11: She said, “No one, Lord.” Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more.”
WEB.12: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world.  He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”
WEB.13: The Pharisees therefore said to him, “You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.”
WEB.14: Jesus answered them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don’t know where I came from, or where I am going.
WEB.15: You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.
WEB.16: Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.
WEB.17: It’s also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.
WEB.18: I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.”
WEB.19: They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me, nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
WEB.20: Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
WEB.21: Jesus said therefore again to them, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can’t come.”
WEB.22: The Jews therefore said, “Will he kill himself, that he says, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come’?”
WEB.23: He said to them, “You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.
WEB.24: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am  he, you will die in your sins.”
WEB.25: They said therefore to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.
WEB.26: I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world.”
WEB.27: They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father.
WEB.28: Jesus therefore said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.
WEB.29: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”
WEB.30: As he spoke these things, many believed in him.
WEB.31: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.
WEB.32: You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”
WEB.33: They answered him, “We are Abraham’s offspring,  and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say, ‘You will be made free’?”
WEB.34: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.
WEB.35: A bondservant doesn’t live in the house forever. A son remains forever.
WEB.36: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
WEB.37: I know that you are Abraham’s offspring,  yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.
WEB.38: I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father.”
WEB.39: They answered him, “Our father is Abraham.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.
WEB.40: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth, which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.
WEB.41: You do the works of your father.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God.”
WEB.42: Therefore Jesus said to them, “If God were your father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven’t come of myself, but he sent me.
WEB.43: Why don’t you understand my speech? Because you can’t hear my word.
WEB.44: You are of your father, the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn’t stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and its father.
WEB.45: But because I tell the truth, you don’t believe me.
WEB.46: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
WEB.47: He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don’t hear, because you are not of God.”
WEB.48: Then the Jews answered him, “Don’t we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?”
WEB.49: Jesus answered, “I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
WEB.50: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.
WEB.51: Most certainly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death.”
WEB.52: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’
WEB.53: Are you greater than our father, Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?”
WEB.54: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.
WEB.55: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him, and keep his word.
WEB.56: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it, and was glad.”
WEB.57: The Jews therefore said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
WEB.58: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM. ”
WEB.59: Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus was hidden, and went out of the temple, having gone through the middle of them, and so passed by.

AMP.1: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
AMP.2: Early in the morning He came back into the temple [court], and all the people were coming to Him. He sat down and began teaching them.
AMP.3: Now the scribes and Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery. They made her stand in the center of the court,
AMP.4: and they said to Him, "Teacher, this woman has been caught in the very act of adultery.
AMP.5: Now in the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women [to death]. So what do You say [to do with her—what is Your sentence]?"
AMP.6: They said this to test Him, hoping that they would have grounds for accusing Him. But Jesus stooped down and began writing on the ground with His finger.
AMP.7: However, when they persisted in questioning Him, He straightened up and said, "He who is without [any] sin among you, let him be the first to throw a stone at her."
AMP.8: Then He stooped down again and started writing on the ground.
AMP.9: They listened [to His reply], and they began to go out one by one, starting with the oldest ones, until He was left alone, with the woman [standing there before Him] in the center of the court.
AMP.10: Straightening up, Jesus said to her, "Woman, where are they? Did no one condemn you?"
AMP.11: She answered, "No one, Lord!" And Jesus said, "I do not condemn you either. Go. From now on sin no more."]
AMP.12: Once more Jesus addressed the crowd. He said, " I am the Light of the world. He who follows Me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the Light of life."
AMP.13: Then the Pharisees told Him, "You are testifying on Your own behalf; Your testimony is not valid."
AMP.14: Jesus replied, "Even if I do testify on My own behalf, My testimony is valid, because I know where I came from and where I am going; but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
AMP.15: You judge according to human standards [just by what you see]. I do not judge anyone.
AMP.16: But even if I do judge, My judgment is true and My decision is right; for I am not alone [in making it], but I and the Father who sent Me [make the same judgment].
AMP.17: Even in your own law it is written that the testimony of two persons is true [valid and admissible].
AMP.18: I am One [of the Two] who testifies about Myself, and My Father who sent Me testifies about Me."
AMP.19: Then the Pharisees said to Him, "Where is this Father of Yours?" Jesus answered, "You know neither Me nor My Father; if you knew Me, you would know My Father also."
AMP.20: Jesus said these things in the treasury, as He taught in the temple [courtyard]; and no one seized Him, because His time had not yet come.
AMP.21: Then He said again to them, "I am going away, and you will look for Me, and you will die [unforgiven and condemned] in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come."
AMP.22: So the Jews were asking [among themselves], "Will He kill Himself? Is that why He says, 'Where I am going, you cannot come'?"
AMP.23: He said to them, "You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.
AMP.24: That is why I told you that you will die [unforgiven and condemned] in your sins; for if you do not believe that I am the One [I claim to be], you will die in your sins."
AMP.25: So they said to Him, "Who are You [anyway]?" Jesus replied, "What have I been saying to you from the beginning?
AMP.26: I have many things to say and judge concerning you, but He who sent Me is true; and I say to the world [only] the things that I have heard from Him."
AMP.27: They did not realize [or have the spiritual insight to understand] that He was speaking to them about the Father.
AMP.28: So Jesus said, "When you lift up the Son of Man [on the cross], you will know then [without any doubt] that I am He, and that I do nothing on My own authority, but I say these things just as My Father taught Me.
AMP.29: And He who sent Me is [always] with Me; He has not left Me alone, because I always do what pleases Him."
AMP.30: As He said these things, many believed in Him.
AMP.31: So Jesus was saying to the Jews who had believed Him, "If you abide in My word [continually obeying My teachings and living in accordance with them, then] you are truly My disciples.
AMP.32: And you will know the truth [regarding salvation], and the truth will set you free [from the penalty of sin]."
AMP.33: They answered Him, "We are Abraham's descendants and have never been enslaved to anyone. What do You mean by saying, 'You will be set free'?"
AMP.34: Jesus answered, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, everyone who practices sin habitually is a slave of sin.
AMP.35: Now the slave does not remain in a household forever; the son [of the master] does remain forever.
AMP.36: So if the Son makes you free, then you are unquestionably free.
AMP.37: I know that you are Abraham's descendants; yet you plan to kill Me, because My word has no place [to grow] in you [and it makes no change in your heart].
AMP.38: I tell the things that I have seen at My Father's side [in His very presence]; so you also do the things that you heard from your father."
AMP.39: They answered, "Abraham is our father." Jesus said to them, "If you are [truly] Abraham's children, then do the works of Abraham and follow his example.
AMP.40: But as it is, you want to kill Me, a Man who has told you the truth, which I heard from God. This is not the way Abraham acted.
AMP.41: You are doing the works of your [own] father." They said to Him, "We are not illegitimate children; we have one [spiritual] Father: God."
AMP.42: Jesus said to them, "If God were your Father [but He is not], you would love and recognize Me, for I came from God [out of His very presence] and have arrived here. For I have not even come on My own initiative [as self-appointed], but He [is the One who] sent Me.
AMP.43: Why do you misunderstand what I am saying? It is because [your spiritual ears are deaf and] you are unable to hear [the truth of] My word.
AMP.44: You are of your father the devil, and it is your will to practice the desires [which are characteristic] of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks what it natural to him, for he is a liar and the father of lies and half-truths.
AMP.45: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe Me [and continue in your unbelief].
AMP.46: Which one of you [has proof and] convicts Me of sin? If I speak truth, why do you not believe Me?
AMP.47: Whoever is of God and belongs to Him hears [the truth of] God's words; for this reason you do not hear them: because you are not of God and you are not in fellowship with Him."
AMP.48: The Jews answered Him, "Are we not right when we say You are a Samaritan and [that You] have a demon [and are under its power]?"
AMP.49: Jesus answered, "I do not have a demon. On the contrary, I honor My Father, and you dishonor Me.
AMP.50: However, I am not seeking glory for Myself. There is One who seeks [glory for Me] and judges [those who dishonor Me].
AMP.51: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, if anyone keeps My word [by living in accordance with My message] he will indeed never, ever see and experience death."
AMP.52: The Jews said to Him, "Now we know that You have a demon [and are under its power]. Abraham died, and also the prophets; yet You say, 'If anyone keeps My word, he will never, ever taste of death.'
AMP.53: Are You greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets died too! Whom do You make Yourself out to be?"
AMP.54: Jesus answered, "If I glorify Myself, My glory is [worth] nothing. It is My Father who glorifies Me, of whom you say, 'He is our God.'
AMP.55: Yet you do not know Him, but I know Him fully. If I said I did not know Him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know Him and keep His word.
AMP.56: Your father Abraham [greatly] rejoiced to see My day (My incarnation). He saw it and was delighted."
AMP.57: Then the Jews said to Him, "You are not even fifty years old, and You [claim to] have seen Abraham?"
AMP.58: Jesus replied, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, before Abraham was born, I Am."
AMP.59: So they picked up stones to throw at Him, but Jesus concealed Himself and left the temple.

MSG.1: Jesus went across to Mount Olives,
MSG.2: but he was soon back in the Temple again. Swarms of people came to him. He sat down and taught them.
MSG.3: The religion scholars and Pharisees led in a woman who had been caught in an act of adultery. They stood her in plain sight of everyone
MSG.4: and said, "Teacher, this woman was caught red-handed in the act of adultery.
MSG.5: Moses, in the Law, gives orders to stone such persons. What do you say?"
MSG.6: They were trying to trap him into saying something incriminating so they could bring charges against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger in the dirt.
MSG.7: They kept at him, badgering him. He straightened up and said, "The sinless one among you, go first: Throw the stone."
MSG.8: Bending down again, he wrote some more in the dirt.
MSG.9: Hearing that, they walked away, one after another, beginning with the oldest. The woman was left alone.
